items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help shopping centers and small businesses get their licenses in order, coupled with outreach and incentives for retail and startups. This could affect local business compliance and may bring previously unlicensed operators into the formal system.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading, moving the project forward. This suggests new housing or commercial space is coming to that location.

items.headline: Mountain View seeks state funding for housing programs
items.summary: The city applied for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program. This could bring state money to support local housing initiatives and affordability efforts.

items.headline: City seeks public input on $2026-27 housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is opening comment on how it will spend federal HUD housing grants for the coming fiscal year. This is your chance to weigh in on affordable housing priorities before the city finalizes the plan.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is holding a public meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss a safe parking program, likely related to parking for unhoused residents or vehicle dwellers. Details on what the program entails and how it will work were not provided in the notice.
